meta.title: Scrapbound Settlement
meta.author: LoreRelay (sample)
meta.description: Post-apocalyptic scavenger loop demo: notice board rumors, ruin runs, unidentified salvage, appraisal, and market trade.

setup.world: Ash years after the collapse. Scrapbound is a walled settlement of traders, fixers, and ruin-runners. Old metro tunnels and dead factories still hide salvage — and worse.
setup.protagonist: A new scavenger with a light pack, a little credit, and no reputation yet.
setup.tone: Gritty but hopeful. Everyday survival, sharp banter, and the thrill of a good haul. Explain prices and risks clearly.
setup.rules: Use 1d20 for risky ruin choices. Commerce and Campaign Kit are enabled. Trade via turn_result.tradeOps. Unidentified salvage stays vague until appraisal.

director.objective: Teach hub → rumor/job → ruin run → salvage → appraisal/sale → world reaction.
successConditions: The player accepts a notice-board job or rumor lead.
The player enters a ruin and returns with at least one finding.
An unidentified item is appraised or sold through the settlement loop.
failConditions: Salvage values are invented without tradeOps or appraisal flow.
Ruins are described without hazard or preparation choices.
optionalEncounters: A fixer offers to fence goods at a suspicious markup.
Raiders shadow the road to the metro entrance.

options: Read the notice board for paid salvage jobs.
Ask the fixer what the north metro rumor is about.
Buy food and a filter mask before leaving town.
opening.narrative:
Scrapbound smells like hot metal, fried oil, and rain on old concrete. Vendors shout over a generator chorus while a chalk board lists jobs: wire from the dead factory, med packs from the clinic bunker, "anything that still boots" from the north metro.

Mira the fixer leans on her counter beside a tray of unidentified scrap. "You look new. Rule one: don't eat mystery cans. Rule two: bring me junk I can sell, or bring me stories I can sell. Rule three: the metro still has lights sometimes. That's never good news."

Your pack is empty, your credits are thin, and the ruins are waiting.
